evidence_quotes.quote: that the Videogames we buy are not ours and that they are free to close them when they want and stay without access to the product you bought to license it
You cannot share your games – you need to be connected to the internet 24 hours of the day or the system is blocked
not to deprive us of that good we pay to buy it
a bed that collects biometrics data

paragraphs.text: The rights of people and consumers of Videogames each time again in decline, now the big companies, those who care more about their investors and to increase their profits without taking into account those we wear where they say that the Videogames we buy are not ours and that they are free to close them when they want and stay without access to the product you bought to license it, as if one month Netflix were treated, it has already happened many times to give the example The Crew, a video game that closes your activity by depriving players of their games on the ground that they do not play a role in their own business, and that they are not able to rely on the internet without playing a role? A person who was buying from the shop and in your previous shop, because you have a precious ‘flatdown’ by the great majority of players, customers, consumers and users only want you to be unable to take it away when you pay for a video game as if you were a subscription (regardless of whether you want to buy it from the convenience of your home in digital format or if you decide to do it in a physical format in order to also help to preserve and use it in the future) if it is true that there are experiences called MMO (Multiplayer Online Masivos) on which you pay for a game that you pay for a single game (World Oway-WORK) – it’s like to create a single game for a single game (World Oy-Side) – it is true that there are MMO (Multiplayer Online Masivos) in which you pay for a game that you pay for a game (World Oway-WORK). The only thing that needs to be demanded is not to deprive us of that good we pay to buy it, to offer alternatives or solutions if that game has a strong online component, to give users a version that is not connected to the network as the vast majority of games have been years ago, nothing new, if your purchase of a video game must be yourself (Nintending for example once I complete its distribution of Animal Crossing on mobile devices, I believe a choice for users who do not require a connection to the internet) many of these cases have been seen but every time and more than those who deprive them of them, with all of them being able to buy what they win. We want our video games to be our. I want to attach an image of when the Microsoft Xbox One console was displayed to the world for the first time, but it is not a format that you accept to tell us what most important (I can corroborate this information easily on the internet) Ideas they got for taps when competition made pro-consumer strategies: You cannot share your games – you need to be connected to the internet 24 hours of the day or the system is blocked, when all your games are no longer supported by the console – you need a bed that collects biometrics data. In the end, this does not apply for the reasons that I have previously commented on, but it is certainly the path that we are slowly approaching with anti-consumer policies applied to us by large video game companies where developers put great effort and passion but managers with suit and dolar in their eyes are responsible for destroying them. Thanks.
